_comment: Add a _ before an option name to comment it out and it wont be added to the apworld
Using 'goal' as the name of an option let you override some of the generated goal parameters
description: Wario Bug - Unlock and clear Anything Goes.
Showdown - Collect every crew member (aside from Pyoro), then unlock and clear Showdown.

filler_traps._comment: Using values here let you set a 'recommended' filler_trap percentage or even a default percentage

description: This is a Toggle
A simple boolean, aka True or False set the initial value with "default"
This setting was removed because it makes gen for a solo game take over 2 minutes.
When True, all modes (aside from Story Mode) are locked initally. You must receive each mode in order to play them.
When True, the goal stage is only accessible after collecting the 3 parts of the Golden Watering Can.
These are the Golden Beehive, the Golden Toilet Paper Roll, and the Golden Holey Mug.
This setting works whether your goal is Showdown or Wario Bug.
Whether to include Play-o-pedia microgames in the randomizer.
Scoring enough to pass the target score on each individual microgame is a check.
Note that this adds, at minimum, 222 checks.
Requires at least one type of check to be specified in PoP_Checks.
Determines how microgames are unlocked. Does nothing if the Play-o-pedia is not included.
'starting' - All microgame checks are available at the start of the game, provided you have the required crew members. (Warning: May add lots of sphere 1 checks!)
'by_category' - Microgames are received by category. For example, receiving 'Play-o-pedia Sports' will unlock all Sports microgames at once.
'individually' - Microgames are unlocked individually.
Whether to include Wario Cup challenges in the randomizer.
'off' - Wario Cup challenges are not included.'
'bronze' - Clearing each challenge with bronze is a check. Adds 52 checks.
'gold' - Clearing each challenge with bronze is a check, and earning a gold trophy on each challenge is an additional check. Adds 104 checks.
Determines how Wario Cup challenges are unlocked. Does nothing if Wario Cup is not included.
'starting' - All Wario Cup challenges are available at the start of the game. Some challenges require certain characters, so not all will be completable immediately.
'individually' - Challenges are unlocked individually.
Whether to include Variety Pack minigames in the randomizer.
'off' - Variety Pack minigames are not included.'
'clears' - Playing each minigame once is a check.
'expert' - Playing each minigame once is a check, and scoring highly enough to clear the minigame's Expert mission is an additional check.
'master' - Playing each minigame once is a check, and scoring highly enough to clear the minigame's Expert and Master missions are an additional 2 checks.
(Scores required for these missions can be seen in the Missions menu ingame, and are also included in the names for the checks.)
Determines how Variety Pack minigames are unlocked. Does nothing if Variety Pack is not included.
'starting' - All Variety Pack minigames are available at the start of the game and will all be sphere 1 checks.
'individually' - Minigames are unlocked individually.
Additional note: Daily Grind currently does not logically require any crew members and can be played as soon as the minigame is unlocked.
Not implemented yet.
Adds additional checks for clearing each Variety Pack minigame with each character. Daily Grind is not included in this option.
'off' - All Variety Pack checks can be done with any character.
'clears' - Clearing each minigame with each character is a check.
'expert' - Playing each minigame once with each character is a check, and scoring highly enough to clear the minigame's Expert mission with each character is an additional check.
'master' - Playing each minigame once with each character is a check, and scoring highly enough to clear the minigame's Expert and Master missions with each character are additional checks.
(Master difficulty is not recommended.)
This is a Choice
Let the user pick from a list of values
allow_custom_value let a player define their own text value
Currently the only use for Choice in Manual outside of hooks is the goal
This is a Range
Allow the player to specify a value between 'start' and 'end'
if you include a "values" you can define the label for certain values
Currently the only use for Range in Manual outside of hooks is 'filler_trap'
